requirements.title: PID parity bit P0
requirements.description: protect_id must compute P0 = ID0 XOR ID1 XOR ID2 XOR ID4

requirements.title: PID parity bit P1
requirements.description: protect_id must compute P1 = NOT(ID1 XOR ID3 XOR ID4 XOR ID5)

requirements.title: Classic checksum covers data only
requirements.description: calc_checksum in Classic mode sums data bytes only (no PID)

requirements.title: Enhanced checksum covers PID plus data
requirements.description: calc_checksum in Enhanced mode sums PID byte plus all data bytes

requirements.title: Carry-around addition
requirements.description: Checksum sum uses carry-around: when sum exceeds 0xFF subtract 0xFF (not 0x100)

requirements.title: Checksum inversion
requirements.description: Final checksum byte is bitwise inverted: result = 0xFF - sum

requirements.title: Frame ID range
requirements.description: Frame ID must be in range 0x00..=0x3F; validate_frame rejects id > 0x3F

requirements.title: Frame data length
requirements.description: Frame data length must be 1 to 8 bytes inclusive

requirements.title: Diagnostic frames require ClassicChecksum
requirements.description: Frames with ID 0x3C or 0x3D must use ClassicChecksum; enhanced is rejected

requirements.title: validate_frame enforces all invariants
requirements.description: validate_frame checks id range, data non-empty, data length ≤ 8, diagnostic checksum type

requirements.title: publish stores slave response
requirements.description: Bus::publish stores data keyed by id; does not broadcast to subscribers

requirements.title: send_header drives frame exchange
requirements.description: MasterBus::send_header computes PID, checksum, validates, and broadcasts to subscribers

requirements.title: set_schedule validates entry IDs
requirements.description: set_schedule rejects any entry with id > 0x3F

requirements.title: NoResponse returned when no slave response registered
requirements.description: send_header returns Error::NoResponse when no response is registered for the requested id

requirements.title: verify_pid rejects incorrect parity
requirements.description: verify_pid returns Err(InvalidFrame) when parity bits in pid do not match computed values

requirements.title: FrameReceiver returns None after close
requirements.description: FrameReceiver::recv returns None once the bus or the receiver is closed

requirements.title: Back-pressure must not block bus sender
requirements.description: When subscriber queue is full, frames are dropped (DropNewest or DropOldest) rather than blocking send_header

requirements.title: Bus close is idempotent
requirements.description: Calling Bus::close more than once returns Ok without error

requirements.title: to_message sets protocol = 3
requirements.description: RELAY adapter to_message must set Message::protocol = Protocol::Lin (3)

requirements.title: from_message rejects wrong protocol
requirements.description: RELAY adapter from_message returns Err(InvalidFrame) when Message::protocol != Lin

requirements.title: NoResponse kind maps to Timeout
requirements.description: Error::NoResponse::kind() must return relay::Error::Timeout (ErrNoResponse IS Timeout)

requirements.title: VirtualBus publish stores SlaveResponse
requirements.description: VirtualBus::publish stores data with Enhanced checksum type by default

requirements.title: VirtualBus publish_classic stores SlaveResponse with Classic type
requirements.description: VirtualBus::publish_classic stores data with Classic checksum type

requirements.title: VirtualBus send_header broadcasts to matching subscribers
requirements.description: send_header delivers frame to all active subscribers whose filters match

requirements.title: VirtualBus metrics tracking
requirements.description: VirtualBus tracks published, delivered, and dropped frame counts atomically

requirements.title: VirtualBus health reporting
requirements.description: VirtualBus::health returns HealthStatus::Healthy when open, HealthStatus::Unhealthy when closed

requirements.title: VirtualBus subscribe returns FrameReceiver
requirements.description: subscribe creates a SubInner and returns a FrameReceiver; the receiver receives frames broadcast by send_header

requirements.title: VirtualBus filter semantics
requirements.description: A subscriber with empty filters receives all frames; a subscriber with filters receives only matching frames

requirements.title: VirtualBus publish None removes slave response
requirements.description: Publishing None for an id removes any existing slave response for that id

requirements.title: VirtualBus set_schedule validates IDs
requirements.description: set_schedule on VirtualBus rejects any entry with id > 0x3F

requirements.title: VirtualBus concurrent safety
requirements.description: Multiple concurrent send_header calls must not race or corrupt state

requirements.title: VirtualBus back-pressure DropOldest
requirements.description: When a subscriber queue is full with DropOldest policy, the oldest frame is dropped and the new frame is enqueued

requirements.title: VirtualBus back-pressure DropNewest
requirements.description: When a subscriber queue is full with DropNewest policy, the new frame is dropped and the queue is unchanged

requirements.title: MasterNode set_schedule validates non-empty
requirements.description: MasterNode::set_schedule rejects an empty schedule with InvalidFrame error

requirements.title: MasterNode set_schedule validates ID range
requirements.description: MasterNode::set_schedule rejects any entry with id > 0x3F

requirements.title: MasterNode run executes schedule loop
requirements.description: MasterNode::run iterates schedule entries, calls send_header for each, invokes on_frame or on_error callbacks

requirements.title: MasterNode run respects context cancellation
requirements.description: MasterNode::run exits its loop when ctx.done() returns true

requirements.title: MasterNode run invokes on_error for NoResponse
requirements.description: When send_header returns NoResponse, run invokes on_error(id, Error::NoResponse)

requirements.title: Oversized payload rejected
requirements.description: Data longer than 8 bytes is rejected by validate_frame

requirements.title: Non-LIN protocol message rejected by from_message
requirements.description: from_message must return Err(InvalidFrame) when msg.protocol != Protocol::Lin (3)

requirements.title: E2E sequence counter replay detected
requirements.description: Receiver::unwrap must return SequenceGap when the received counter is not exactly last_seq+1

requirements.title: LDF parser must not panic on malformed input
requirements.description: ldf::parse must return Ok(Db) for any input — empty, truncated, or hostile — without panicking

requirements.title: E2E header too short rejected
requirements.description: Receiver::unwrap must return HeaderTooShort when data.len() < 10

requirements.title: No unsafe code in library or binary
requirements.description: No unsafe blocks exist in src/; rsfusa lint enforces this property on every CI run

requirements.title: Invalid PID rejected
requirements.description: verify_pid rejects PIDs with mismatched parity bits

requirements.title: ID overflow rejected
requirements.description: Frame IDs > 0x3F are rejected by validate_frame and send_header

requirements.title: Parse extracts protocol version
requirements.description: ldf::parse shall populate Db::protocol_version from LIN_protocol_version field

requirements.title: Parse extracts baud rate
requirements.description: ldf::parse shall populate Db::speed_kbps from LIN_speed field

requirements.title: Parse extracts master node name
requirements.description: ldf::parse shall populate Db::master_node from Nodes::Master declaration

requirements.title: Parse extracts slave node list
requirements.description: ldf::parse shall populate Db::slave_nodes from Nodes::Slaves declaration

requirements.title: Parse populates frame descriptors
requirements.description: ldf::parse shall populate frame id, name, publisher, and length from Frames section

requirements.title: Parse populates signal-to-bit-offset mappings
requirements.description: ldf::parse shall populate SignalRef entries in each frame from Frames section

requirements.title: Parse populates signal bit width
requirements.description: ldf::parse shall populate Signal::bit_width from Signals section

requirements.title: Parse populates signal publisher
requirements.description: ldf::parse shall populate Signal::publisher from Signals section

requirements.title: Decode uses LSB-first Intel byte order
requirements.description: Db::decode shall extract signal bits using LSB-first (Intel) byte ordering

requirements.title: Decode returns None for unknown frame ID
requirements.description: Db::decode shall return None when the frame ID is not in the LDF

requirements.title: Parse populates schedule table entries
requirements.description: ldf::parse shall populate schedule table name-to-entries mappings from Schedule_tables section

requirements.title: frame() returns None for unknown ID
requirements.description: Db::frame(id) shall return None when the frame ID is not present

requirements.title: signal() returns None for unknown name
requirements.description: Db::signal(name) shall return None when the signal name is not present

requirements.title: Parse does not panic on arbitrary input
requirements.description: ldf::parse shall not panic on malformed or empty input; it returns Ok(Db) with partial data

requirements.title: frames() returns a defensive copy
requirements.description: Db::frames() returns a HashMap clone; mutations do not affect the internal Db state

requirements.title: DataID embedded in header bytes 0-1
requirements.description: Protector::protect shall write DataID as little-endian u16 at header bytes 0–1

requirements.title: SourceID embedded in header bytes 2-3
requirements.description: Protector::protect shall write SourceID as little-endian u16 at header bytes 2–3

requirements.title: SequenceCounter starts at 0 and increments
requirements.description: Protector SequenceCounter starts at 0 and atomically increments by 1 per protect() call

requirements.title: SequenceCounter embedded in header bytes 4-7
requirements.description: Protector::protect shall write SequenceCounter as little-endian u32 at header bytes 4–7

requirements.title: CRC computed over header and payload
requirements.description: CRC-16/CCITT-FALSE (poly=0x1021, init=0xFFFF) computed over header (CRC slot zeroed) + payload

requirements.title: CRC embedded in header bytes 8-9
requirements.description: Protector::protect shall write CRC as little-endian u16 at header bytes 8–9

requirements.title: Unwrap returns E2eError::HeaderTooShort for payload < 10 bytes
requirements.description: Receiver::unwrap returns E2eError{kind:HeaderTooShort} when data.len() < 10

requirements.title: Unwrap detects byte corruption via CRC
requirements.description: Receiver::unwrap returns E2eError{kind:CrcMismatch} when CRC check fails

requirements.title: Unwrap detects sequence gap
requirements.description: Receiver::unwrap returns E2eError{kind:SequenceGap} when received counter != last+1

requirements.title: Unwrap returns original payload on success
requirements.description: Receiver::unwrap returns the original payload bytes on successful validation

requirements.title: Protect/Unwrap round-trip preserves payload
requirements.description: protect(payload) followed by unwrap(protected) shall return the original payload unchanged

requirements.title: Protect output length equals 10 + len(payload)
requirements.description: Protector::protect shall return exactly 10 + len(payload) bytes

requirements.title: Unwrap accepts first message with any counter value
requirements.description: Receiver::unwrap shall accept any counter value on the first call to seed the sequence

requirements.title: Protect is safe for concurrent calls
requirements.description: Protector::protect may be called from multiple threads concurrently without data races

requirements.title: Unwrap returns an independent payload copy
requirements.description: Receiver::unwrap returns an owned Vec<u8>; mutations to the returned buffer do not affect internal state

requirements.title: New returns ready slave node
requirements.description: SlaveNode::new wraps a Bus and returns a node with no registered responses

requirements.title: SetResponse registers response via Publish
requirements.description: SlaveNode::set_response calls Bus::publish to register the response payload

requirements.title: SetResponse(None) removes registration
requirements.description: SlaveNode::set_response with None calls Bus::publish(id, None) and removes the ID from registered_ids

requirements.title: SetResponse rejects ID > MaxID
requirements.description: SlaveNode::set_response returns Error::InvalidFrame when id > 0x3F

requirements.title: RegisteredIDs reflects current state
requirements.description: SlaveNode::registered_ids returns a sorted list of all currently registered frame IDs

requirements.title: Subscribe delegates to bus
requirements.description: SlaveNode::subscribe delegates to the underlying Bus::subscribe

requirements.title: RegisteredIDs returns empty slice when none registered
requirements.description: SlaveNode::registered_ids returns an empty Vec when no responses are registered

requirements.title: SetResponse overwrites previous registration for same ID
requirements.description: A second set_response call for the same ID overwrites the previous registration; registered_ids still shows the ID once

requirements.title: Integrating system provides physical LIN layer
requirements.description: Assumption: the integrating system provides an ISO 17987-compliant physical LIN layer

requirements.title: Integrating system calls validate_frame on external data
requirements.description: Assumption: the integrating system calls validate_frame on any frame received from an external LIN interface

requirements.title: Integrating system validates frame ID semantics
requirements.description: Assumption: the integrating system enforces application-level frame ID semantics beyond the 6-bit range

requirements.title: Integration: virtual bus delivers E2E payload intact
requirements.description: A protect/unwrap round-trip over the virtual bus preserves the original payload

requirements.title: Integration: master-slave round-trip via virtual bus
requirements.description: A full master-slave publish/send_header/subscribe cycle delivers the registered payload to the subscriber

requirements.title: Integration: LDF schedule IDs are valid
requirements.description: All frame IDs derived from an LDF-parsed schedule must be valid LIN IDs (≤ 0x3F)

requirements.title: Integrating system handles ErrNoResponse safely
requirements.description: Assumption: the integrating system treats Error::NoResponse as a recoverable condition and does not propagate it as a fault

requirements.title: Integrating system routes safety-critical frames through safety package
requirements.description: Assumption: the integrating system applies safety::Protector/Receiver to payloads requiring E2E protection

requirements.title: Integrating system provides monotonic clock for timing
requirements.description: Assumption: the integrating system provides a monotonic clock when timing LIN schedule slot delays

requirements.title: Adapt returns a LIN relay::Node
requirements.description: adapt() wraps a Bus and returns a Box<dyn relay::Node> with Protocol::Lin

requirements.title: LinAdapter::send publishes payload for a valid frame ID
requirements.description: relay::Node::send calls Bus::publish with the parsed frame ID and payload

requirements.title: LinAdapter::send rejects an out-of-range frame ID
requirements.description: relay::Node::send returns relay::Error on non-numeric or out-of-range Message::id

requirements.title: LinAdapter::subscribe converts frames to relay::Message
requirements.description: relay::Node::subscribe returns a channel of relay::Message envelopes with Protocol::Lin and correct meta fields

requirements.title: LinAdapter::close closes the underlying bus
requirements.description: relay::Node::close delegates to Bus::close

requirements.title: mock::MockBus satisfies Bus and MasterBus
requirements.description: MockBus implements Bus and MasterBus; it records published responses and returns them from send_header
